commit.message:
🧹 Code Health: Split large SettingsScreen composable (#129)

* 🧹 refactor: Split large SettingsScreen composable into smaller sections

Extracted AppearanceSection, LibrarySection, ReaderSection, NotificationsSection, and BackupRestoreSection from the monolithic SettingsScreen to improve readability and maintainability. Localized specific states (e.g. grid slider) into their respective child composables while delegating events correctly to the top level.

* chore: remove CodeQL advanced config workflow

Removed `.github/workflows/codeql.yml` because the repository currently relies on the default CodeQL setup, causing a conflict and failing the CI check ("CodeQL analyses from advanced configurations cannot be processed when the default setup is enabled").

* Update feature/settings/src/main/java/app/otakureader/feature/settings/SettingsScreen.kt

commit.message:
fix: cap cloud futures to prevent GPU phase starvation

During GPU phases, _submit_io_jobs claimed unlimited cloud_ai jobs into a
1-thread ThreadPoolExecutor. With copilot (1 worker, ~10s/job), thousands
of futures accumulated. When the phase ended, ThreadPoolExecutor.shutdown()
blocked for hours waiting for the backlog, preventing blip2/faces/embedding
from advancing to the next phase.

Fix:
- Track _cloud_in_flight counter in _submit_io_jobs
- Cap to effective_cloud_workers * 2 (2 for copilot)
- Decrement in _collect_futures and _cancel_futures
- Cancel uncompleted futures at phase boundaries so the pool shuts down
  promptly; cancelled jobs return to pending for the IO drain phase

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add Nord Pool price-optimized charging schedule

Fetches hourly prices from Nord Pool API (cached 1h), selects the
cheapest hours within the charging window to concentrate charging.

Algorithm:
- Calculate hours needed at minimum rate (6A) with 20% buffer
- Rank remaining hours by price, pick cheapest N
- If current hour is cheap → charge at deadline rate
- If current hour is expensive AND charger hasn't started → wait
- If charger already running during expensive hour → continue at min rate
- Fallback: if API unavailable, reverts to spread-evenly strategy

Example: 5 kWh needed over 8h window → picks cheapest 2h out of 8,
charges during 02:00-04:00 (0.65 SEK/kWh) instead of 22:00 (0.83 SEK)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

  provision-copilot.ps1, check-test-readiness.ps1, complete-test-setup.ps1,

  (prevents broken images in network-restricted tenants)

- Add network prerequisite note to demo-walkthrough.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

build: reduce wheel size via OpenBLAS DYNAMIC_LIST + NO_CBLAS + strip

- Add DYNAMIC_LIST to OpenBLAS builds: generic uses NEHALEM..ZEN3
  (drops pre-2010 arches like BARCELONA/CORE2); AVX2 uses HASWELL..ZEN3.
  CPUs not in the list fall back to the generic kernel automatically.
- Add NO_CBLAS=1: COIN-OR calls Fortran-style BLAS (dgemm_ etc.) so
  the cblas_* C wrappers are dead weight.
- Add _strip_binaries(): runs strip --strip-unneeded (Linux) / strip -x
  (macOS) on all shared libs and executables after each release build.
  Debug builds are intentionally not stripped.

Expected savings: ~30-40% off OpenBLAS, ~30% off COIN-OR libs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Centralize CPU feature detection in SimdCapabilities singleton

- Fix NativeAOT: use IlcInstructionSet (not InstructionSet) for ILC compiler
- Use .NET 10 bundled instruction set names: avx2,fma,bmi2,lzcnt
- Expand SimdCapabilities: add Sse42X64, ArmCrc32Arm64, Vector128/256/512
  Accelerated, VectorBitWidth, Crc32CPath, GetMismatchWarnings()
- Dashboard (MetricsTracker): read all features from SimdCapabilities.Current
  instead of direct IsSupported calls (which are AOT compile-time constants)
- DataLayerCapabilities.Crc32CPath: delegate to SimdCapabilities.Crc32CPath
- Startup log: emit mismatch warnings when CPUID disagrees with runtime
- Hot-path dispatch (SimdDistance, SimdVectorMath, DictionaryColumnFilter,
  WalCrc32C) intentionally keeps direct IsSupported checks — ILC requires
  them to dead-code-eliminate unused instruction paths

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: consolidation banner stays dismissed until consolidation runs

Add consolidation_dismissed flag. When user clicks Dismiss, set both
suggest_consolidation=false and consolidation_dismissed=true. The
UtxosUpdated handler now checks !consolidation_dismissed before re-raising
the banner, so it won't pop back every poll tick. The flag is cleared on
ConsolidationComplete so the banner can reappear if still needed after
a consolidation run.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: stabilize flaky action menu test on Windows Node 20 (#401)

* fix: stabilize flaky action menu test on Windows Node 20

Add waitForFrame helper to wait for expected content instead of fixed
delays. The 'action menu View logs opens inline log viewer' test was
flaky on Windows Node 20 because 50ms delays were insufficient for the
event loop to process UI updates between keystrokes.

Changes:
- Add waitForFrame() helper with configurable timeout and poll interval
- Replace fixed delays with content-based waits for dashboard render,
  action menu appearance, and log viewer appearance
- Increase arrow-key delays to 100ms for menu navigation

Fixes #385

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: replace remaining timing delays with cursor-based waits

Replace the three `delay(100)` calls in the action menu navigation test
with `waitForFrame` calls that poll for the cursor position after each
arrow key press:
- After 1st down-arrow: wait for '❯ Open in browser'
- After 2nd down-arrow: wait for '❯ Attach to session'
- After 3rd down-arrow: wait for '❯ View logs'

Also fix waitForFrame to strip ANSI escape codes before matching, since
Ink renders colored/styled text that interferes with string matching.
Add mkdirSync for WORKTREE_DIR so the 'Attach to session' option appears.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: split notification emails into realtime alerts and periodic digests

Replace the single notification email system (4 frequencies: immediately,
delayed, daily, weekly) with 2 distinct email types:

Realtime notification emails:
- Sent when important events occur (relation requests, limit changes, etc.)
- 15-minute buffer to batch rapid changes
- Minimum 1-hour interval between emails per user
- Independent opt-in/opt-out toggle

Periodic digest emails:
- Daily or weekly summary (user's choice)
- Activity summary section (new relations, limits, pending requests)
- Excludes notifications already sent via realtime email (24h window)
- Minimum 20-hour interval guard

Schema changes:
- notification_email_settings: replaced frequency/delayHours/weeklyDays with
  digestEnabled/digestFrequency/digestTime/digestWeeklyDay/realtimeEnabled
- Added email_notification_log table for deduplication tracking
- Data migration preserves existing user preferences

New files:
- notificationClassifier.ts: priority classification for notification types
- Realtime notification email template (HTML + text)
- Enhanced digest template with activity summary section

Updated:
- notificationScheduler.ts: split into processRealtimeEmails() + processDigestEmails()
- Profile settings API: new GET/PUT format for dual email types
- Frontend: two toggle sections (realtime alerts + periodic digest)
- Tests: rewritten for new architecture (53 tests passing)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
docs: team consensus on tool priorities — voted and assigned

Team voting ceremony completed. All 25 tools from Jango's proposal
prioritized by squad consensus based on:
- Which M1+M2 blockers each tool would have prevented
- Which agents explicitly requested similar functionality
- Impact on developer satisfaction (8/10 cited 'never saw my work')

Sprint breakdown:
- Sprint A (7 tools): Critical blockers prevention, before M3
- Sprint B (6 tools): Developer joy, during M3
- Sprint C (8 tools): Process automation, post M3
- Backlog (4 tools): Deferred

Top 3 priorities:
1. Integration Gate Automation (#33) — 17 points
2. Godot Headless Validator (#35) — 15 points
3. Branch Validation CI (#34) — 14 points

Key decision: Test Scene Generator moved from Sprint B to Sprint A
due to overwhelming team demand (80% reported satisfaction gap).

Next: Create remaining 18 GitHub issues for Sprint A/B/C tools.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add missing Invalidate() calls to FileInfo state-changing methods (#124429)

Fixes #117709 — FileInfo methods that alter file system state now call
Invalidate() so cached properties (Exists, Length, timestamps) are
lazily refreshed on next access, matching the pattern already used by
Create(), Delete(), and MoveTo().

Methods fixed (FileInfo.cs):
  - Open(FileStreamOptions)
  - Open(FileMode, FileAccess, FileShare)
  - OpenRead()
  - OpenWrite()
  - OpenText()
  - Replace(string, string?)
  - Encrypt()
  - Decrypt()
  - CreateAsHardLink(string)

Comprehensive method audit across FileInfo, DirectoryInfo,
FileSystemInfo:

  FileInfo — already correct (no change needed):
    Create(), Delete(), MoveTo(), CreateText(), AppendText(),
    CopyTo() (returns new obj), IsReadOnly setter (via Attributes)

  DirectoryInfo — already correct (no change needed):
    Create(), Delete(), Delete(bool), MoveTo()
    CreateSubdirectory() returns new obj, parent unchanged

  FileSystemInfo — already correct (no change needed):
    CreateAsSymbolicLink(), Attributes setter, CreationTime(Utc) setter,
    LastAccessTime(Utc) setter, LastWriteTime(Utc) setter,
    UnixFileMode setter, ResolveLinkTarget() (read-only)

  Read-only methods (no invalidation needed):
    GetFiles, GetDirectories, GetFileSystemInfos, Enumerate*

Includes 12 regression tests in InvalidateOnStateChange.cs covering all
fixed methods plus CreateText/AppendText (already worked).

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: direct polygon passthrough for table row citation highlighting

Instead of relying on fragile geometry-distribution + text-matching
pipeline to link table row polygons, cell bounding_region polygons
are now extracted and normalized directly in _extract_table_metadata()
and stored alongside each row dict as _polygons/_page.

extract_sentences_from_di_units() Source B reads _polygons directly,
bypassing _match_geometry_for_sentence(). The text-matching path is
retained as fallback. Warnings logged when neither path provides data.

All row.items() consumers (schema_aware_extractor, synthesis) filter
_-prefixed internal keys to prevent noise in LLM context.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- DashboardScreen: hero status card with glow + live server uname display
- CommandsScreen: monospace cmd preview, run/live buttons, edit/delete
- JobsScreen: cron schedule chips, next/last run timestamps, edit/delete
- ServersScreen: ping ms display, full uname panel, probe/re-probe flow
- CommonComponents: StatusDot pulse animation, GoldGradientCard, chips

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

chore: bump version to v0.7.4

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: remove max-w-xs so project path is not truncated in status bar

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(hm-client): add edit dialogs for commands, jobs, and servers

All three resource types support in-place editing via POST with same ID
(daemon uses INSERT OR REPLACE). Edit opens a pre-filled dialog.

- CommandsScreen: edit dialog pre-fills id/name/command/workDir/timeout
- JobsScreen: edit dialog pre-fills id/name/schedule/commandId
- ServersScreen: edit dialog pre-fills id/name/host; shows uname/ping
- CommonComponents: add shared HmTextField and HmErrorText components

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: keep function routes indexed when dependency imports fail

Wrap shared module imports in startup-safe guards so the app can still index HTTP routes
instead of returning blanket 404s when optional runtime dependencies fail to import.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(hm-client): fix Gson plaintext error on /status endpoint

- Change getStatus() return type to Response<ResponseBody> to handle
  plain-text 'Daemon Operational' response without Gson parse error
- Configure Gson with setLenient() in ApiClient for robustness
- DashboardViewModel reads body as raw string via .body()?.string()

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add manual-tests.sh and fix ARCHITECTURE.md section numbering

- tests/manual-tests.sh: cut-and-paste reference for all 5 server
  topologies (QEMU+mlx5, QEMU+i40e, Firecracker, container, native),
  matching vaigai commands, traffic commands, monitoring, and cleanup
- docs/ARCHITECTURE.md: fix duplicate section 3.7 (renumber Mem Stats
  to 3.8, Structured Logging to 3.9), add manual-tests.sh to test list

commit.message:
docs: Colima/OrbStack do NOT support ASAN on Apple Silicon

Docker Desktop (Rosetta 2) is the only macOS ARM64 Docker runtime that
supports ASAN shadow memory mappings. Colima uses QEMU and OrbStack
uses Virtualization.framework — neither can handle ASAN shadow memory,
so ASAN-instrumented binaries crash at runtime. Updated all docs to
warn macOS agents to use Docker Desktop only.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Refactor: consolidate formatters, extract shared components, standardize patterns (#12)

* Refactor: consolidate formatters, extract shared components, standardize patterns

- Merge formatCurrency/formatCurrency2 into single function with decimals param
- Extract formatPctRaw, formatCompactCurrency to lib/format.ts (DRY)
- Share MONTHS_SHORT constant across date formatters
- Create ChartTooltip component to deduplicate 5 chart tooltip implementations
- Centralize MCP response extraction to lib/mcp-response.ts
- Create DashboardCard component to replace 10+ inline card class strings
- Create lib/api-utils.ts with errorResponse() for consistent API error handling
- Add useActiveResult() selector hook to Zustand store
- Simplify store usage with destructured selectors in dashboard/navigator
- Fix pre-existing react-hooks/immutability lint error in equity-curve.tsx
- Remove dead tool-existence checks in signals/strategies routes
